mnemonics: CAP the Environment: Cluster, Android, Parallel. Think of putting a cap on different computing environments to manage them.
keyPoints: A computing environment is the overall setting where a user runs programs and an OS manages resources.
Traditional environments include mainframe/client-server systems, while modern ones include distributed, clustered, and handheld systems.
The choice of environment (e.g., clustered for high availability, real-time for timing constraints) depends on the application's specific needs.
commonMistakes: Assuming a single-user workstation OS (like Windows) has the same design goals as a clustered OS (like for a data center).
Confusing distributed systems (loosely coupled nodes) with parallel systems (tightly coupled multiprocessors).
